Title: Complete biosynthesis of opioids in yeast

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Growth medium of engineered yeast cultures
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: With T6ODM, enabled production of hydrocodone from thebaine-derived intermediates in engineered yeast
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. morB was introduced with T6ODM as a hydrocodone extension module. 2. The combined module enabled detectable hydrocodone production from sugar in yeast. 3. Final hydrocodone titers remained very low (~0.3 µg/L).
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway extension in yeast; LC-MS/MS detection of hydrocodone
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 50 mM 2-oxoglutarate supplementation for paired T6ODM step
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory enzyme for engineered downstream opioid diversification
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classed as common gene here because paper emphasizes pathway extension rather than detailed enzyme mechanism

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Epimerizes (S)-reticuline to (R)-reticuline via oxidation to 1,2-dehydroreticuline followed by stereospecific reduction
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Reticuline epimerization step leading to promorphinan/morphinan branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies direct precursor for morphinan alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Analgesic/pain management and palliative care medicines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Growth medium of engineered yeast cultures
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables conversion of (S)-reticuline to (R)-reticuline and supports downstream production of salutaridine, thebaine, and hydrocodone
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bioinformatic analysis identified a fused CYP82Y1-like/COR-like enzyme as the missing reticuline epimerase candidate. 2. Expression of Pbr.89405 in engineered yeast shifted reticuline from exclusively (S) to more than half (R). 3. P. bracteatum and P. somniferum variants supported thebaine production in the reconstructed pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step; required gateway from (S)-reticuline to the morphinan branch
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Fusion protein containing cytochrome P450–like domain and COR-like reductase domain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Bioinformatic discovery; heterologous expression in yeast; chiral LC-MS analysis; feeding assays; downstream metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fed rac-norlaudanosoline or (via pathway) reticuline-producing conditions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Plant transcriptome mining using 1000 Plants Project and PhytoMetaSyn databases
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential module for reconstructing morphinan biosynthesis in microbes; should be included when engineering de novo opioid pathways from (S)-reticuline
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Natural fusion architecture suggested coupled oxidation and reduction chemistry; identified independently by multiple groups around the same time

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts (R)-reticuline to salutaridine as the first committed cytochrome P450 step toward the morphinan scaffold
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor toward thebaine and hydrocodone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Analgesic/pain management and palliative care medicines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Growth medium of engineered yeast cultures
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes the key conversion of (R)-reticuline to salutaridine; engineered chimeras markedly improved thebaine pathway flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Wild-type SalSyn was inefficient in yeast and accumulated as misprocessed glycosylated forms. 2. Glycosylation-site mutation alone did not improve function. 3. Chimeric engineering with CFS N-termini repaired processing and increased salutaridine formation about sixfold and thebaine production more than threefold.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Bottleneck enzyme / key control point in reconstructed pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Western blot; site-directed mutagenesis; chimera engineering; feeding assays with (R)-reticuline and rac-norlaudanosoline; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Misprocessing and N-linked glycosylation in yeast reduced activity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major engineering target for improving microbial opioid production; N-terminal redesign/chimera strategies are valuable for heterologous plant P450 deployment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Best variant was yEcCFS1-83-yPbSalSyn92-504; BM3-based fusion was also tested

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Analgesic/pain management and palliative care medicines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Growth medium of engineered yeast cultures
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports conversion of salutaridine toward thebaine; P. bracteatum variant showed highest activity in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SalR variants from P. bracteatum and P. somniferum were screened in yeast. 2. The P. bracteatum enzyme exhibited the highest activity in the reconstructed pathway. 3. PbSalR was incorporated into the optimized thebaine module.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Variant screening in yeast; feeding assays; LC-MS/MS analysis of downstream products
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reported substrate inhibition addressed by mutant variants in preliminary testing
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream enzyme choice for optimized microbial thebaine synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PbSalR selected for final pathway design

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: opium poppy; Iranian poppy; P. orientale
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Papaver somniferum; Papaver bracteatum; Papaver orientale
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: salutaridinol-7-O-acetate leading to spontaneous/enzymatic rearrangement to thebaine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates salutaridinol at the 7-O position in thebaine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursor to thebaine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Growth medium of engineered yeast cultures
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes thebaine formation; P. somniferum variant showed highest activity in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SalAT variants from multiple Papaver species were compared. 2. The P. somniferum enzyme exhibited the highest activity in yeast. 3. PsSalAT was used in the final thebaine module.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Variant screening in yeast; pathway feeding assays; LC-MS/MS detection of thebaine
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful terminal-step enzyme choice for microbial thebaine production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Selected as best-performing variant for final pathway

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Demethylates thebaine at the 6-O position in the engineered hydrocodone route
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Growth medium of engineered yeast cultures
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled extension of the pathway beyond thebaine to hydrocodone when coexpressed with morB
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Introduction of T6ODM with morB extended the reconstructed pathway beyond thebaine. 2. Engineered yeast produced low levels of hydrocodone. 3. T6ODM activity required 2-oxoglutarate supplementation in culture.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway extension in yeast; culture supplementation; LC-MS/MS detection of hydrocodone
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Very low final hydrocodone titer in current strains
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for extending microbial production to semisynthetic opioid products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used in a route not native to opium poppy when paired with morB
